title: Aulomyrcia garopabensis
intro: Aulomyrcia garopabensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae, commonly known as a shrub or small tree native to the Atlantic Forest in Brazil. The health benefits of Aulomyrcia garopabensis include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Aulomyrcia garopabensis include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
